info.name: SupportFlow Server — API Test Suite
info.description:
Auto-generated Postman collection covering every endpoint actually exposed by SupportFlow-server (auth, business, agents, customer, superadmin).

Usage:
1. Import this file into Postman.
2. Open the collection variables (Variables tab) — set BASE_URL if your server is not on http://localhost:3000.
3. Run requests top-to-bottom in each folder. Tokens and IDs chain automatically into collection variables.
4. For OTP-gated steps (verify-otp, reset-password) set otp_A / otp_B / resetOtp_A manually from the email/log output before running the request.
5. Superadmin folder requires a manually seeded superadmin (see folder description).

exec: // Collection-level baseline assertions (run after every request)
pm.test('No passwordHash leak in response', function () {

item.name: 01 — Auth (Happy Path)
item.description: Registration → OTP verification → Login → /me. Run in order. After 'Register Admin A/B', open the OTP from your email or server logs and paste it into the otp_A / otp_B collection variable, then run the matching 'Verify OTP' request.

item.name: Verify OTP (Admin A) — paste otp_A first
Verify OTP (Admin B) — paste otp_B first
Refresh Access Token
item.description: Uses the refreshToken httpOnly cookie set by the login response. Postman keeps cookies per domain automatically.

item.name: Reset Password (Admin A) — paste resetOtp_A first
Google Login (template — needs real idToken)
item.description: Replace 'PASTE_GOOGLE_ID_TOKEN' with a real Google ID token. Will return 503 if GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID is not configured server-side.

item.name: 02 — Auth (Negative Tests)
item.description: Validation failures, auth failures, and duplicate-email guards.

item.name: 03 — Business / Workspace
item.description: Create the workspace tied to each verified admin account. Both admin tokens (A and B) must be set first.

exec: pm.test('business name = Alpha Corp Support', function () { pm.expect(json.data.business.name).to.eql('Alpha Corp Support'); });
pm.test('Different business id from A', function () { pm.expect(json.data.business._id).to.not.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get('businessId_A')); });

item.name: [NEGATIVE] Create Business — already owned
item.description: Re-running create business with the same admin token should be rejected because user already belongs to a business.

item.name: 04 — Agent Management
item.description: Admin-only CRUD on agents under the same business. Newly created agents are auto-verified, so they can log in directly without OTP.

item.name: [ISOLATION] Business B cannot list Business A agents
item.description: Listing agents with B's admin token must not include any of A's agents.

exec: pm.test('No Business A agentId leak', function () {

item.name: [ISOLATION] Business B cannot update Business A agent
05 — Customer (Public Widget)
item.description: Public endpoint used by the in-app widget to identify a visitor against a business workspace.

item.name: 06 — SuperAdmin
item.description: Requires a user with role='superadmin'. The /auth/register endpoint cannot create one — seed manually in Mongo (db.users.updateOne({email:'super@platform.com'},{$set:{role:'superadmin', isEmailVerified:true}})) or via a seed script. Then run 'Login Superadmin' first to populate {{superToken}}. Adjust superadminEmail/superadminPassword variables as needed.

item.name: Toggle Business B (deactivate then reactivate by re-running)
